title: podman machine ssh bad configuration option on Windows
body:
### Issue Description

After updating from 4.7.2 to 5.0.2, `podman machine ssh` fails on Windows

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine reset -f`
2. `podman machine init --now`
3. `podman machine ssh`


### Describe the results you received

`podman machine ssh` fails on Windows with the following error:
```
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
command-line: line 0: Bad configuration option: setenv
```

### Describe the results you expected

An SSH session with the podman machine

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows Version:
```
> systeminfo | findstr /B /C:"OS Name" /B /C:"OS Version"
OS Name:                   Microsoft Windows 10 Enterprise
OS Version:                10.0.19045 N/A Build 19045
```

### Additional information

Downgrading to Podman version 4.9.4 fixes the issue so it does seem to be a 5.x.x problem, and not something between 4.7.x and 4.9.x.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Aetylus What version of ssh do you have on your Windows box?

> @Aetylus What version of ssh do you have on your Windows box?

I'm on 7.6p1:
```
> ssh -V
OpenSSH_7.6p1, OpenSSL 1.0.2m  2 Nov 2017
```
I've updated to OpenSSH 8.1p1 and also to Podman 5.1.1 and this no longer appears to be an issue
